perturbation.original_text: (c) Receiving Party agrees that for a period of twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, unless specifically consented to in writing by Blueknight, Receiving Party and its controlled Affiliates will not, directly or indirectly, effect any sale (including any short-sale) or acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or acquisition of assets of Blueknight or any of its Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Receiving Party agrees that Receiving Party and its controlled Affiliates will not, directly or indirectly, effect any sale (including any short-sale) or acquisition of any equity securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or acquisition of assets of Blueknight or any of its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'for a period of twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, unless specifically consented to in writing by Blueknight', the agreement creates an open ended restriction on the Receiving Party from selling or acquiring securities. The original text had a definite timeline, but the change forces the receiving party to have no ability to sell or acquire securities. Since there are other clauses referencing this part, it creates a contradiction that affects the enforcement of the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder (other than Section 4(b)) shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (i) twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, (ii) such time as Receiving Party is no longer in possession of Blueknight Non-public Information and (iii) with respect to any Section of this Agreement (other than Section 4(b)), such earlier time as set forth in such Section. Section 4(b) of this Agreement and the obligations of the parties under Section 4(b) shall terminate upon the date that is ten (10) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the restriction on selling/acquiring securities to 20 days, as stated in clause (c) of the contradicted text. The changed text removes this time limit, imposing a potentially indefinite restriction. This directly contradicts clause (c) in the contradicted text, which states the agreement terminates after 20 days from the Refinancing Meeting. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Each of Blueknight, the General Partner and Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that each of the parties shall be entitled, without the requirement of posting a bond or other security, to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, the non-prevailing party as determined by a court of competent jurisdiction in a final judgment shall be liable and pay to the prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses such prevailing party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Blueknight, the General Partner and Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that each of the parties shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, the non-prevailing party as determined by a court of competent jurisdiction in a final judgment shall be liable and pay to the prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses such prevailing party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without the requirement of posting a bond or other security', from the clause detailing the remedy for any breach in the agreement, there is an assumption that a bond or security must be required now. Section 5(b) clearly outlines that all parties are entitled to specific performance, injunctive, or other equitable relief without needing to post a bond or security. By removing this key part in the definition, it creates a contradiction that changes the enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of Blueknight, the General Partner and Receiving Party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that each of the parties shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, the non-prevailing party as determined by a court of competent jurisdiction in a final judgment shall be liable and pay to the prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses such prevailing party has incurred in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "without the requirement of posting a bond or other security" from the original text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that parties are entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief without needing to post a bond or security. By removing this phrase, the changed text implies that a bond or security may now be required, directly conflicting with the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it changes the conditions under which specific performance and injunctive relief can be sought.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder (other than Section 4(b)) shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (i) twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, (ii) such time as Receiving Party is no longer in possession of Blueknight Non-public Information and (iii) with respect to any Section of this Agreement (other than Section 4(b)), such earlier time as set forth in such Section. Section 4(b) of this Agreement and the obligations of the parties under Section 4(b) shall terminate upon the date that is ten (10) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (i) twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, (ii) such time as Receiving Party is no longer in possession of Blueknight Non-public Information and (iii) with respect to any Section of this Agreement, such earlier time as set forth in such Section. Section 4(b) of this Agreement and the obligations of the parties under Section 4(b) shall terminate upon the date that is ten (10) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception clause '(other than Section 4(b))' from the initial part of the sentence, and removing the same clause from part (iii) of the sentence, and retaining the last sentence as is, the change introduces a contradiction regarding the termination of the agreement. The removal of those parts indicates that the obligations of the parties under Section 4(b) *do* terminate based on points (i), (ii), or (iii). However, the final sentence states that Section 4(b)'s obligations terminate ten years from the date, which is now in direct conflict. This contradictory language about termination dates significantly impacts the long-term enforcement of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (i) twenty (20) days from the date of the Refinancing Meeting, (ii) such time as Receiving Party is no longer in possession of Blueknight Non-public Information and (iii) with respect to any Section of this Agreement, such earlier time as set forth in such Section. Section 4(b) of this Agreement and the obligations of the parties under Section 4(b) shall terminate upon the date that is ten (10) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes Section 4(b) from the termination conditions outlined in points (i), (ii), and (iii), ensuring its termination is solely governed by the ten-year clause. The changed text removes this exclusion, making Section 4(b) subject to the earlier termination conditions of points (i), (ii), and (iii). This directly contradicts the final sentence, which states Section 4(b)'s obligations terminate ten years from the date. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text in this case, but since the change causes a contradiction with the final sentence regarding the termination date of Section 4(b), it is a clear contradiction. The explanation clearly demonstrates how the change creates the contradiction.
